originalTitle: ‘We defeated Los Ingleses’: Argentina fans search for consolation as Messi misses out on perfect ending
summary: Fans in Buenos Aires head to Obelisco monument to celebrate a semi-final victory over England and honour what looks like Lionel Messi’s last World Cup
content: “What happened?” people outside an empanada shop in downtown Buenos Aires ask, trying to read the groans and gestures of the customers inside whose TV was a minute ahead of theirs. “Spain scored,” says Julián Escalante, 20, holding up his phone. Spain’s Ferran Torres had delivered the decisive blow in the 106th minute of a final that had gone into extra time, sealing a tense, largely one-sided showdown that ended 1-0. When the final whistle blew, Escalante doesn’t hesitate and reminds the other supporters: “We defeated Los Ingleses [the English], man! And that’s what matters.” Escalante, a stretcher-bearer, says Argentina’s team “was a bit all over the place, but it doesn’t matter – they gave it everything they had. We made it to the final. We made it to extra time. That’s because we’ve got huevos.” “This is the first time I’ve felt so proud of my country,” he says, heading toward the Obelisco, the designated meeting point to celebrate Argentina’s efforts. “We beat France in the final [in 2022]. We have three World Cups. Messi retired from the Argentina national team as the greatest player in the world.” Cintia Martínez, 28, a housekeeper, can barely hold back tears. “I don’t want to speak much, because my son is crying and I will too,” she says, holding her four-year-old’s hand. Also heading to the Obelisco, she says she was celebrating anyway, “because we made it to the final”. Thousands did the same and went to the monument. The 67-metre-high Obelisco was lit up with images of the Argentinian squad and its supporters, amid the sound of fireworks nearby. A street vendor sold Fernet, a spirit, with coke: “To wash the anger down!” Next to the Obelisco, Ellen Vicente, 20, a Brazilian medical student wearing an Argentina jersey, says she came out because she respects Argentinians and considers it “an honour” to live in the country. “We are not Argentine, but you played very well, and I’m very proud,” she says. Asked about recent racism accusations against Argentinians, she said they’re partly substantiated. “I am Black, and it is real, but we can’t generalise – this country has welcomed us, and most people treat us well,” she adds. “And Argentina deserved to win.” Emiliano Romero, 18, hung an Argentine banner on the Obelisco’s fence. It read: “Thank you! You made a people happy. Love is paid with love.” Romero explains: “This is love for our country, something I can’t explain. Football is like that – you win, and you lose. But we’re proud of the result all the same.” Young people sat along the fence of the Obelisco. “We’re climbing up here because of the euphoria of the moment, of wanting to be seen, of the love all Argentines have for one another,” said Sofía Gómez, 22, a shop assistant. “Those who watched the match knew that, for Leo Messi’s last game, it had to be Argentina.”
chineseTitle: “我们击败了洛杉矶队”：梅西错失完美结局，阿根廷球迷寻求安慰
chineseSummary: 布宜诺斯艾利斯的球迷前往方尖碑纪念碑庆祝半决赛战胜英格兰队，并纪念莱昂内尔·梅西的最后一届世界杯
chineseContent: “发生了什么？”布宜诺斯艾利斯市中心一家肉馅卷饼店外的人们问道，试图读懂里面顾客的呻吟声和手势，他们的电视比他们的电视早一分钟。 “西班牙进球了，”20 岁的朱利安·埃斯卡兰特 (Julian Escalante) 举起手机说道。决赛进入加时赛，西班牙队的费兰·托雷斯在第 106 分钟打出决定性一击，锁定了一场紧张且一边倒的对决，最终以 1-0 告终。当终场哨声响起时，埃斯卡兰特毫不犹豫地提醒其他支持者：“我们击败了Los Ingleses（英格兰人），伙计！这才是最重要的。”担架手埃斯卡兰特表示，阿根廷队“有点混乱，但这并不重要——他们竭尽全力。我们进入了决赛。我们进入了加时赛。那是因为我们有huevos。” “这是我第一次为我的国家感到如此自豪，”他一边说，一边走向方尖碑，这是庆祝阿根廷努力的指定聚会点。 “我们在[2022年]决赛中击败了法国队。我们已经三届世界杯了。梅西作为世界上最伟大的球员从阿根廷国家队退役。” 28 岁的管家辛蒂亚·马丁内斯 (Cintia Martínez) 几乎忍不住流下了眼泪。 “我不想多说话，因为我儿子在哭，我也会哭，”她握着四岁孩子的手说道。她也前往方尖碑，表示无论如何她都在庆祝，“因为我们进入了决赛”。数千人也这样做了，前往纪念碑。在附近的烟花声中，67 米高的方尖碑被阿根廷队及其支持者的图像照亮。一名街头小贩将一种烈酒 Fernet 与可乐一起出售：“可以冲淡愤怒！”在方尖碑旁边，20 岁的巴西医科学生埃伦·维森特 (Ellen Vicente) 身穿阿根廷球衣，她说她出来是因为尊重阿尔杰
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
